title: Freeze item + subitem header when viewing subitems
excerpt: When scrolling through a long list of subitems, the name of the parent item is no longer visible. This limits us in seeing essential information. Is there a way to freeze that row when adding new subitems?

posts.author: cmarth (Cady Marth)
posts.cookedHtml: When scrolling through a long list of subitems, the name of the parent item is no longer visible. This limits us in seeing essential information. Is there a way to freeze that row when adding new subitems?

posts.author: kdallons (Kat Dallons)
posts.cookedHtml:
Screen Shot 2022-05-13 at 5.23.24 PM1446×616 224 KB

Please add this if it’s not a feature that I’m just missing! We work with many subitems and it’s a little ridiculous to have to keep scrolling up and down to see what columns we’re editing. This would be immensely useful and seems like a standard feature that should be implemented. TY!

posts.author: JParks (Jeff Parks)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. Please add this feature. Using subitems is unwieldy without it.

posts.author: DylanDowns (Dylan Downs)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m surprised this still hasn’t been done. It’s even worse now because the subitem column headings aren’t frozen, but the columns of the main item ARE. So when you scroll down, you see the column headings of the main item against the subitems, resulting in the wrong heading names above the subitem columns!

posts.author: adrien (Adrien)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is indeed confusing. Here are more screenshots to explain the problem:

  1. Screenshot before scrolling where both the table headers and subitem headers are displayed. Note that the table and subitem columns have the same headers but are not aligned.

  2. Screenshot after scrolling. Only the table header is displayed as the subitem header scrolls with the subitems. Hence the header shown does not match the columns displayed. Here the total number of hours appears as if it would be the number of PTO hours.

Monday.com table before scrolling1461×439 41.3 KB

Monday.com table after scrolling1485×263 25.2 KB

posts.author: CaraO (Cara Oba)
posts.cookedHtml: I think we may be adding comments to a different request than what we are asking for. I have just created a new feature request to freeze the header for the subitems (not the parent item) and hopefully it will be posted soon.

posts.author: CaraO (Cara Oba)
posts.cookedHtml: When scrolling through a long list of subitems, the header row for the subitems are no longer visible. It would be very helpful if that header row could be frozen so the user knows what columns they are entering data for. Otherwise, the user needs to scroll up to check the column header and scroll back down. This is particularly frustrating since each new row is added to the bottom.

posts.author: DrewBeFree (Drew Webb)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love to see this as well!

posts.author: ysilva (Yuri)
posts.cookedHtml:
It seems kind of easy to implement. Please dev team, help us out.

thanks

posts.author: l27 (John Long)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would be nice to freeze both item+sub item header. Make it toggleable. Add it under the pinned columns menu as an additional check box. Pin Item when expanded and Pin subitem header. When you scroll past the last subitem, the pinned item that was expanded scrolls out of view too.

Monday270×683 22.7 KB

posts.author: pjtgrizzly (Reid)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed - this seems like a no brainer. Freeze the main parent item at top when long subitems scroll - who would object? as well as the subitem columns frozen at top as one scrolls down through a ton of subitems. Standard stuff

posts.author: l27 (John Long)
posts.cookedHtml: I just used the mobile app for the first time last night. Guess what, the subitem header row is frozen by default. @Mondaydevs please make this happen in the web version!

posts.author: PDBragg (Peter Bragg)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be very helpful. Please add this feature.

posts.author: Kouji (Maverick Ho)
posts.cookedHtml:
Countless time need to scroll up just to check the subitem header then scroll back down to key in value. This is so unproductive!!!

please have implement this feature soonest

posts.author: RobKingofBauer (Robert King)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is still a major issue we feel, at our organisation.

Lots of our boards use subitems, every time you scroll you can only see the parent item columns which are completely different from the subitems.

Makes the subitems pretty unusable and a danger to capture incorrect data.

Please can the monday com Development team take this on board?
